A Most Wanted Man (2014)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $17,237,855Details
International Box Office $18,831,035
Worldwide Box Office $36,068,890
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $1,628,375
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $727,434
Total $2,355,809
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Hamburg, Germany: 2012. A mysterious, tortured and near-dead half-Chechen, half-Russian man on the run arrives in the city’s Islamic community desperate for help and looking to recover his late Russian father’s ill-gotten fortune. Nothing about this young man seems to add up; is he a victim or a thief or, worse still, an extremist intent on destruction? Drawn into this web of intrigue are a banker and a young female lawyer who is determined to defend the defenseless. All the while, they are being watched by the brilliant, roguish chief of a covert German spy unit, who fights to put the pieces together as the clock ticks.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$2,686,526 (15.6% of total gross)
Legs:5.32 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:47.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$15,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:361 opening theaters/808 max. theaters, 5.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $25,234,368

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: July 25th, 2014 (Limited) by Roadside Attractions
International Releases: August 1st, 2014 (Wide) (Australia)
Video Release: November 4th, 2014 by Lionsgate Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for language.
(Rating bulletin 2326, 6/11/2014)
Running Time: 121 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Digital Cinematography, Intertitle, Posthumous Release, Prologue
Plot point: C.I.A., Confidential Informant, Government Corruption, Human Rights, Immigration, Inheritance, Lawyers, Muslims, Secret Police, Surprise Twist, Terrorism, Tortured for Information
Subgenre: Political Thriller
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Lionsgate, Demarest Films, FilmNation Entertainment, Senator Film, Ink Factory, Amusement Park
Production Countries: United Kingdom
Languages: English

Synopsis

Hamburg, Germany: 2012. A mysterious, tortured and near-dead half-Chechen, half-Russian man on the run arrives in the city’s Islamic community desperate for help and looking to recover his late Russian father’s ill-gotten fortune. Nothing about this young man seems to add up; is he a victim or a thief or, worse still, an extremist intent on destruction? Drawn into this web of intrigue are a banker and a young female lawyer who is determined to defend the defenseless. All the while, they are being watched by the brilliant, roguish chief of a covert German spy unit, who fights to put the pieces together as the clock ticks.
